 
Abstract— Image enhancement is a critical step in digital 
image processing, aimed at improving the visual appearance 
or making images more suitable for analysis. This paper 
explores the application of the Fourier Transform (FT) for 
image enhancement. The FT is a powerful mathematical tool 
that decomposes an image into its sinusoidal components. By 
manipulating the frequency domain, significant improvements 
in image quality, edge sharpness, and noise reduction can be 
achieved. The research presents various techniques, 
implementation steps, and the benefits of frequency-based 
image enhancement, particularly focusing on the removal of 
periodic noise and enhancement of image details. 
 
INDEX TERMS— KEYWORDS 
Image Enhancement, Fourier Transform, Frequency Domain, 
Noise Removal, Edge Detection. 
 
 
I. INTRODUCTION 
Digital image processing involves techniques to 
improve image quality for better human perception or 
machine interpretation. Image enhancement focuses on 
highlighting certain features of interest in an image or 
suppressing unwanted distortions. Spatial domain methods 
modify pixel values directly, while frequency domain 
methods, such as those using the Fourier Transform, work on 
the image's frequency content, offering unique advantages in 
processing periodic patterns and reducing noise. 
 
II Image Storage in the Spatial Domain 
Digital images are commonly stored in the spatial domain, 
where each pixel’s position directly corresponds to its 
intensity value. An image is represented as a 2D array 
f(x,y)f(x, y)f(x,y), where xxx and yyy represent the spatial 
coordinates and the value at each location signifies the 
brightness or color of that pixel. Spatial domain methods 
manipulate these pixel values directly to perform operations 
like contrast enhancement, sharpening, and blurring. 
 
 
 
 
III  FOURIER TRANSFORM IN IMAGE PROCESSING 
The Fourier Transform converts spatial domain images into 
the frequency domain, allowing us to analyze the image content 
based on frequency components. High frequencies represent 
edges and fine details, while low frequencies indicate smooth 
variations like background lighting Fig. 1.  
Image Enhancement using Fourier Transform

3.2 Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) 
FFT is an efficient implementation of DFT, significantly 
reducing computation time, which is critical for real-time 
processing.

III. MATH 
  

3 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
B. Algorithms 
1. To analyze the frequency components of a grayscale 
image, the image is first converted to the frequency domain 
using the 2D Discrete Fourier Transform (DFT). This 
process transforms the image from the spatial domain—where 
pixel intensities are directly interpreted—to the frequency 
domain, where each point represents a particular frequency 
contained in the spatial image. 
 
2. Applied high Pass filter  
 
A 
high-pass 
filter 
(HPF) 
allows 
high-frequency 
components to pass through while attenuating or blocking low-
frequency components such as gradual changes in intensity. 
This makes the filter particularly effective in edge 
enhancement and highlighting fine details in an image. 
3. Can Also Apply low pass filter  
 
A low-pass filter (LPF) allows low-frequency 
components to pass through while attenuating the 
high-frequency components of the image. This 

4 
 
operation smoothens the image by reducing sharp 
transitions such as noise and edges. 
 
4.Enhancing Filter Response by Adding a Constant (0.75) 
 
To refine the performance of both high-pass and low-pass 
filters, a constant value (e.g., 0.75) can be added to the filter 
function. This technique is used to tune the filter's strength 
or to avoid extreme suppression of certain frequency 
components. Such adjustments help preserve important visual 
details that might otherwise be lost.
